title: Data Not Syncing
description: Fix cloud sync issues, offline queue problems, and data consistency.

sections.body: If your plant data isn't showing up on the web dashboard or across devices, here's how to diagnose and fix sync issues.

sections.heading: Check Your Connection
sections.body:
Cloud sync requires an active internet connection. The app only pushes data to Turso when navigator.onLine (web) or network reachability (mobile) returns true.

  • Check your WiFi or cellular connection.
  • Try opening a webpage to confirm internet access.
  • If you're on a restricted network (corporate, hotel), the Turso API endpoint may be blocked.

sections.heading: Offline Queue
sections.body:
When you're offline, all sensor readings and care logs are stored locally and queued for sync. When connectivity returns, the app replays the queue automatically. If data seems missing after going back online:

  1. Wait 30 seconds — the sync may still be in progress.
  2. Pull down on the garden screen to trigger a manual sync.
  3. Check if the data appears in the local app but not on the web — this confirms it's a sync issue, not a sensor issue.

sections.heading: Duplicate Prevention
sections.body: Sensor readings have a unique constraint on (sensor_id, timestamp). If two phones capture the same BLE reading, the database uses INSERT OR IGNORE to keep just one copy. This is normal behavior, not data loss.

sections.heading: Data Appears on One Device but Not Another
sections.body:
Sync is push-then-pull: your device pushes its local data first, then pulls updates. If data is on your phone but not the web:

  • Make sure you're logged into the same account on both devices.
  • Check that the push completed — look for any error notifications in the app.
  • Refresh the web dashboard (it doesn't auto-refresh).

sections.heading: Turso Service Status
sections.body: If sync fails consistently, the Turso cloud service may be experiencing issues. The Turso HTTP pipeline API (v2/pipeline) is what Plantgotchi uses to batch SQL operations. If this endpoint is unreachable, all sync operations will fail gracefully and retry later.
